--- SOURCE 1: Congestion Management Solutions | StreetLight Data ---
URL: https://www.streetlightdata.com/traffic-data-congestion-management/

SUMMARY:
The Northern Louisiana Coalition of Governments (NLCOG) wanted to reduce congestion, improve travel time, and upgrade road safety for their regional multimodal transportation network. Case studies, data-driven research, and timely articles to learn how StreetLight helps solve transportation safety challenges. Congestion management solutions include a variety of traffic planning and operations strategies, infrastructure improvements, and targeted policies aimed at improving traffic flow in a given area. Data-driven congestion management solutions can leverage historical and/or real-time traffic insights to go beyond anecdotal evidence to understand traffic trends during any time period (time of day, day of week, or time of year) and on any road segment to understand where and when delays occur.

# Congestion management solutions

## Minimize congestion hotspots and get traffic moving

Identify bottlenecks with targeted solutions using temporal and geographic insight across the entire roadway network. Fast-track congestion management processes (CMP) and improve roadway performance, safety, and mobility.

[Get started](https://www.streetlightdata.com/get-started/)

## Solve bottlenecks with quick access to multi-year traffic insights for all your roads in one easy-to-use platform

Deploy innovative congestion management strategies and fill data gaps on local and rural roads.

1

Save time and money

Get segment-level congestion metrics and spot speeds for all roads without relying on sensors or surveys.

2

Faster reporting

Access recent and historical data to compare roadway performance across multiple years.

3

Resolve public frustrations

Address constituents’ requests for traffic calming measures with the most recent travel conditions.

## Fast-track projects with quick access to roadway performance metrics

[Get started](https://www.streetlightdata.com/get-started/)

Congestion Management Process (CMP)

Analyze traffic congestion patterns with performance measures of travel time reliability, delay, VMT, and segment or spot speed.

Safety Planning

Improve conditions for all road users by analyzing network performance, speeds, and multimodal trip activity to inform congestion mitigation.

Regional Planning

Prioritize statewide and regional transportation plans by quantifying bottlenecks and speeding conditions.

Before-and-After Studies

Evaluate the effectiveness of mitigation measures and transportation policies using data spanning multiple years.

## Prioritizing congestion mitigation across 168 miles of roadway

[Read success story](https://learn.streetlightdata.com/devising-congestion-management-strategies-with-analytics?)

The challenge

The Northern Louisiana Coalition of Governments (NLCOG) wanted to reduce congestion, improve travel time, and upgrade road safety for their regional multimodal transportation network. Planners needed to determine where, why, and when traffic flow was slowing down.

Data-based solution

StreetLight provided traffic flow metrics and helped NLCOG planners integrate data for use in a GIS environment. Planners were able to pinpoint six miles of roadway out of the 168 studied with severe congestion, allowing them to prioritize improvements—especially congestion that impacted transit services.

## StreetLight Congestion Management: At a glance

[Get started](https://www.streetlightdata.com/get-started/)

Congestion metrics

Get mobility metrics, including VMT, delay hours, and segment & spot speeds throughout the corridor for effective roadway network performance assessment.

AADT

Access automated and validated traffic counts for multiple years.

Origin-destination

Understand where traffic is coming from and going to for improved planning.

Up-to-date, multi-year data

Measure existing mobility conditions with recent and historical data years.

Reliability metrics

Analyze traffic trends on corridors with performance reliability metrics, including travel time index, planning time index, and reliability factor.

High-impact visualizations

Get fast takeaways on roadway network performance with day parts and day types granularity, charts, and 3D visualizations.

### FAQs

## Quick answers to questions you may have

Discover everything about StreetLight’s Congestion Management product features, benefits, and more.

What are congestion management solutions?

Congestion management solutions include a variety of traffic planning and operations strategies, infrastructure improvements, and targeted policies aimed at improving traffic flow in a given area. Goals of congestion management often include reducing commuter travel times, eliminating gridlock throughout the road network, or preparing for special events or construction projects that may impact travel demand or road capacity.

What are the key benefits of using data-driven congestion management solutions?

Data-driven congestion management solutions can leverage historical and/or real-time traffic insights to go beyond anecdotal evidence to understand traffic trends during any time period (time of day, day of week, or time of year) and on any road segment to understand where and when delays occur. This data enables a more accurate diagnosis of the causes of slow-downs, the ability to understand how delays ripple out through the entire road network, and the ability to evaluate how potential solutions will impact travel time, mobility, and safety.

How can congestion management software improve traffic flow and reduce delays?

This type of software allows transportation professionals to analyze traffic patterns and explore solutions like retiming signals, promoting public transit, implementing congestion pricing, or establishing alternate routes. It also supports evaluating the success of past projects and helps communicate the value of new projects to stakeholders, ensuring smoother operations and gaining necessary support.

How granular is the congestion management data?

The StreetLight InSight® platform allows you to view congestion analytics based on day types, day parts, and peak hours. It covers all roadway classifications, including rural and urban roads, as well as residential and highway routes.

## Do you need data to inform your next project?

--- SOURCE 3: acog congestion management process (cmp) ---

SUMMARY:
The CMP is designed to develop and implement strategies to manage and reduce congestion throughout the transportation system. By taking a data-driven approach,

FULL CONTENT:
![Association of Central Oklahoma Governments](https://www.acogok.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/ACOG-Full-Wordmark-Logo-Blue-1.png)
![](https://www.acogok.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/CMP-Website-Header-2025.png "CMP Website Header 2025")

#### acog congestion management process (cmp)

## Moving Forward: Smart Solutions for a Less Congested Future

### **What is Congestion Management?**

Congestion Management is the application of strategies to improve transportation system performance and reliability by reducing congestion’s adverse effects on the movement of people and goods. A well-managed transportation system enhances mobility, economic growth, and quality of life.

### **Purpose of the CMP**

The CMP is designed to develop and implement strategies to manage and reduce congestion throughout the transportation system. By taking a data-driven approach, the CMP identifies solutions that enhance efficiency, improve safety, and support long-term regional growth.

### **The CMP Process**

The Congestion Management Process is an ongoing, systematic method of identifying congestion challenges and developing solutions. It provides accurate, up-to-date information on transportation system performance and evaluates alternative strategies to address congestion-related issues.

The CMP is an integral part of ACOG transportation planning efforts and plays a key role in project selection for the **Long-Range Transportation Plan (LRTP)** and **Transportation Improvement Program (TIP).**

### **Federal Requirements**

As a federally mandated process, all metropolitan areas with populations exceeding 200,000 are required to develop a Congestion Management Process. ACOG follows an **8-Step Process/Model** to ensure compliance and effectiveness.

# **CMP Goals**

ACOG evaluates its CMP through performance-based measures aligned with **five national transportation goals**:  
✔ **Safety** – Enhancing roadway safety for all users  
✔ **Infrastructure Condition** – Maintaining a state of good repair for transportation assets  
✔ **Congestion Reduction** – Reducing travel delays and improving efficiency  
✔ **System Reliability** – Enhancing consistency in travel times  
✔ **Freight Movement & Economic Vitality** – Supporting the efficient movement of goods and regional economic growth

## What Does the CMP Achieve Long-Term?

The CMP ensures that congestion management strategies move from planning to action. By identifying, assessing, and funding solutions, the CMP helps create a more reliable, safe, and efficient transportation network for Central Oklahoma.

--- SOURCE 4: 10 Smart Approaches To Resolving Road Traffic Problems - Navata ---
URL: https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/

SUMMARY:
Traffic problems need smart solutions that make transportation systems work better while protecting our environment and saving time. Robust public transportation networks in developed countries prove that effective transportation systems help reduce traffic problems and lower pollution levels while making urban travel better for everyone. The new traffic routes spread out vehicle movements across all streets which reduces vehicle backups at main roads.Cities need to build more secondary roads while setting up special lanes for trucks and buses to move through town.When cities add more public transportation services to their sustainable transportation options people find it easier to switch from private cars. When cities link park-and-ride facilities to public transportation they create better ways for people to commute sustainably while decreasing traffic and supporting ecological urban development.

10 Smart Approaches to Resolving Road Traffic Problems
======================================================

#### Table of Contents

1.   [Smart Approaches to Resolving Road Traffic Problems](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-0) 
2.   [1. Promoting Public Transportation](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-1) 
3.   [2. Carpooling Initiatives](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-2) 
4.   [3. Modernizing Infrastructure](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-3) 
5.   [4. Non-Motorized Transport](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-4) 
6.   [5. Intelligent Traffic Management Systems](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-5) 
7.   [6. Use of Alternative Routes](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-6) 
8.   [7. Developing Park-and-Ride Facilities](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-7) 
9.   [8. Supporting Electric and Autonomous Vehicles](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-8) 
10.   [9. Raising Awareness and Education](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-9) 
11.   [10. Develop a Comprehensive Light Rail Network](https://navata.com/cms/road-traffic-problems/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-10) 

Smart Approaches to Resolving Road Traffic Problems
---------------------------------------------------

Traffic jams create major problems in cities throughout the world that affect how people live and how cities operate. The problem slows down travel times and uses more fuel while creating environmental hazards that harm both the planet and human health. Heavy traffic slows down the delivery of goods and services which reduces business output.

Populated cities and rising vehicle numbers make the situation worse. Traffic problems need smart solutions that make transportation systems work better while protecting our environment and saving time. Through smart approaches and tech-enabled policies, we can solve transportation difficulties to make our systems more efficient and sustainable. Here are ten smart approaches to resolving road traffic problems:

![Image 2: Road Traffic Problems](https://navata.com/cms/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/NRT-4-2.webp)

You May Also Like to Read: [Drones Delivery a Sustainable Future for Transportation?](https://navata.com/cms/drones-delivery-a-sustainable-future/)

1. Promoting Public Transportation
----------------------------------

When people use public buses trains and metro systems they take fewer private cars off the roads. When cities invest in better public transportation systems people choose this mode of travel over their cars. When public transit fares stay low and connecting services run smoothly they make people want to use their cars less.

Technology tools that show vehicle locations and let people pay online make traveling simpler. Robust public transportation networks in developed countries prove that effective transportation systems help reduce traffic problems and lower pollution levels while making urban travel better for everyone.

2. Carpooling Initiatives
-------------------------

When people share rides in a single vehicle they help lower the number of cars traveling together. Users can link up with other travelers headed the same way through mobile apps and special websites. Sharing rides reduces road congestion and saves money on fuel while cutting back on pollution.

Entities like governments and businesses help carpooling grow by giving participants special deals including reduced toll fees and tax reductions plus exclusive carpooling lanes. Carpooling programs in the United States and India demonstrate how they help people save money while preserving our environment.

3. Modernizing Infrastructure
-----------------------------

Building new roads, bridges, and flyovers is a tried-and-true method of reducing traffic congestion in densely populated places. However, modernizing is equally important. Smart traffic infrastructure, such as adaptive traffic signals and IoT-enabled sensors, improves traffic flow.

Upgrading roads with adequate signs, markings, and upkeep may improve safety and efficiency. Urban planning measures, such as bypass and ring highways, direct traffic away from city cores. Investment in well-designed infrastructure greatly decreases traffic congestion and enhances passenger experiences.

4. Non-Motorized Transport
--------------------------

Our community needs better transportation choices between cycling and walking to decrease car use. More people prefer walking and biking because safer dedicated paths are now available for these modes of travel. Governments back non-motorized transport through bike-sharing plans along with bicycle purchase discounts and safe bike storage construction.

Using transportation without motors helps both reduce traffic jams and enables people to live healthier with cleaner air. Through these efforts, cities benefit when they invest in cycling networks to decrease traffic and create better neighborhoods.

5. Intelligent Traffic Management Systems
-----------------------------------------

Traffic congestion is a major issue in many cities, causing delays, frustration, and increased pollution. One solution is to revolutionize traffic light management using sensors and GPS data. AI-driven systems optimize traffic flow, reduce delays, and cut emissions by minimizing idling at red lights. Real-time adjustments can respond to changing traffic conditions.

Technologies like LYT’s transit signal priority and emergency vehicle preemption allow traffic signals to communicate in real-time, improving efficiency. As more cities adopt intelligent traffic management systems (ITMS), congestion will decrease, with better traffic flow, smarter route suggestions, and automated toll collection to enhance road efficiency.

6. Use of Alternative Routes
----------------------------

Traffic jams have become a global city issue that creates travel delays and discomfort while hurting business.Using alternative routes represents an effective way to address this problem.Cities display traffic updates about roads with low congestion through their GPS systems traffic apps and electronic displays.

The new traffic routes spread out vehicle movements across all streets which reduces vehicle backups at main roads.Cities need to build more secondary roads while setting up special lanes for trucks and buses to move through town.When cities add more public transportation services to their sustainable transportation options people find it easier to switch from private cars.

You May Also Like to Read: [Challenges in Indian Transportation Industry 2025](https://navata.com/cms/challenges-in-indian-transportation-industry-2025/)

7. Developing Park-and-Ride Facilities
--------------------------------------

People can leave their vehicles in parking spaces near city limits and ride public transportation from there to their destinations through park-and-ride systems. When fewer cars enter city traffic it makes roads less crowded and creates cleaner air.

These facilities work best when you create safe parking areas charge reasonable rates and connect them to dependable public transport systems. When cities link park-and-ride facilities to public transportation they create better ways for people to commute sustainably while decreasing traffic and supporting ecological urban development.

8. Supporting Electric and Autonomous Vehicles
----------------------------------------------

Electric cars (EVs) and self-driving vehicles (AVs) are transforming the transportation business. EVs assist in minimizing greenhouse gas emissions and dependency on fossil fuels, while AVs improve traffic flow through smart routing and vehicle-to-vehicle communication.

To speed up the adoption of these technologies, governments might provide subsidies for EV purchases, invest in broad charging infrastructure, and implement policies that encourage innovation and growth. These initiatives not only promote sustainable transportation, but they also open the path for cleaner, more efficient urban transportation networks, resulting in a considerable decrease in environmental impact and increased mobility.

You May Also Like to Read: [Electric Trucks on The Road: Regulations, Incentives, and the Future of Trucking](https://navata.com/cms/electric-trucks-on-the-road/)

9. Raising Awareness and Education
----------------------------------

Public awareness and education efforts are essential in encouraging ethical road usage and sustainable commuting practices. Highlighting the advantages of carpooling, public transportation, and non-motorized transportation might inspire people to make ecologically responsible decisions.

Workshops, social media campaigns, and school programs may help individuals understand the value of traffic safety and the environmental effect of their commuting choices. Successful awareness campaigns demonstrate how education may promote a culture of responsible, sustainable mobility.

10. Develop a Comprehensive Light Rail Network
----------------------------------------------

Building a comprehensive light rail network is an efficient way to reduce traffic congestion. Light rail runs on dedicated rails, typically with its own right-of-way, away from other vehicles, making it a faster and more efficient means of transportation than buses, which often become caught in traffic.

Light rail vehicles can transport more passengers at once, reducing traffic congestion. Integrating a light rail system into the current mass transport network improves connectivity and expands people’s travel alternatives. This complete system not only decreases traffic but also encourages sustainable transportation options, therefore supporting environmental goals and improving overall mobility.

Thank You For Reading: 10 Smart Approaches to Resolving Road Traffic Problems

enriched_points.expanded_content: Increasing traffic congestion leads to longer travel times, impacting daily commutes and logistics. Effective congestion management strategies are vital to enhance mobility and reduce delays.
Insufficient funding hampers transport network enhancements. Strategic investment in infrastructure development is essential to maintain and modernize transportation systems for future needs.

Transport networks contribute significantly to carbon emissions. Transitioning to eco-friendly alternatives, such as electric vehicles and improved public transport, is crucial for sustainable urban development.
Many transport systems are burdened by outdated infrastructure and limited capacity. Upgrading facilities and expanding capacity are necessary to meet increasing demand and ensure safety.
